verses.text: Ten virgins take lamps; five wise carry oil, five foolish none; all slumber as the bridegroom tarries. Inward readiness. The wise have what the foolish lack — the oil of true grace.
Ten virgins take lamps; five wise carry oil, five foolish none; all slumber as the bridegroom tarries. Inward readiness. The wise have what the foolish lack — the oil of true grace.

Ten virgins take lamps; five wise carry oil, five foolish none; all slumber as the bridegroom tarries. Inward readiness. The wise have what the foolish lack — the oil of true grace.
At midnight the cry comes; the foolish beg oil, but the wise cannot spare it. Borrowed grace fails. None can lend another the readiness of his own soul.

The ready enter, the door is shut; 'Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour.' Too late. The unprepared find the door closed against them.

A man gives his servants five, two, and one talent; the first two trade, the last hides his. Stewardship. God entrusts to each according to ability, and looks for increase.

To the faithful the lord says, 'Well done... enter thou into the joy of thy lord.' Sweet welcome. Faithful use of God's gifts is met with His own joy.

The one-talent servant hid it, calling his lord hard; 'thou wicked and slothful servant.' Slander and sloth. He blames the master to excuse his own idleness.
The one-talent servant hid it, calling his lord hard; 'thou wicked and slothful servant.' Slander and sloth. He blames the master to excuse his own idleness.

The one-talent servant hid it, calling his lord hard; 'thou wicked and slothful servant.' Slander and sloth. He blames the master to excuse his own idleness.
His talent is given to him with ten, and he is cast into outer darkness. Use it or lose it. The gift unused is taken away, and the slothful condemned.

When the Son of man comes in glory, He divides the nations as a shepherd parts sheep from goats. The great assize. All shall be gathered and separated before Him.

'Come, ye blessed... inherit the kingdom'; I was hungry and ye fed me, a stranger and ye took me in. Faith's fruit. Mercy to the needy marks the truly His.
'Come, ye blessed... inherit the kingdom'; I was hungry and ye fed me, a stranger and ye took me in. Faith's fruit. Mercy to the needy marks the truly His.
'Come, ye blessed... inherit the kingdom'; I was hungry and ye fed me, a stranger and ye took me in. Faith's fruit. Mercy to the needy marks the truly His.
When saw we thee? 'Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.' Christ in the needy. Kindness to His own is reckoned as done to Him.
When saw we thee? 'Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.' Christ in the needy. Kindness to His own is reckoned as done to Him.

'Depart... ye cursed'; I was hungry and ye fed me not, a stranger and ye took me not in. Neglect condemned. The wasted life of mercy is exposed at the judgment.
'Depart... ye cursed'; I was hungry and ye fed me not, a stranger and ye took me not in. Neglect condemned. The wasted life of mercy is exposed at the judgment.
'Depart... ye cursed'; I was hungry and ye fed me not, a stranger and ye took me not in. Neglect condemned. The wasted life of mercy is exposed at the judgment.
When saw we thee and helped not? as ye did it not to the least, ye did it not to me; they go to everlasting punishment. Two destinies. The careless and the merciful part for ever.
